content.text:
# Umberhold Monsters

This folder contains individual monster entries for Umberhold.

---

## Monster Index

| Monster | Level | Size | Type | Notes |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| [Boggart Knifer](./boggart-knifer.md) | 1 | Small | Humanoid | Early weak skirmisher with a rare unique drop: **Boggart Cloak** |
| [Wulf](./wulf.md) | 1 | Small | Beast | Mangy pack beast with a diseased bite and a rare **Bone Collar** drop |

---

## Notes

- Use `umberhold/templates/monster-template.md` for new monster entries.
- Core monster rules are tracked in `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`.
- XP rules are tracked in `umberhold/experience-canon.md`.

# Umberhold Item List

This is a growing master item list for Umberhold.

It can include:
- mundane items
- magical items
- unique drops
- quest items
- consumables
- armor and weapons

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## Items

| Name | Type | Magical | Weight | Effect | Source / Notes |
|---|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Boggart Cloak** | Cloak / Armor Accessory | No | 1 stone | Grants advantage on Stealth checks | Rare unique drop from **Boggart Knifer** (1 in 500). A patchwork cloak of filthy cloth, flayed skin, and scraps of wulf fur, reeking of damp rot. |

# Umberhold Items

This folder contains individual item entries for Umberhold.

---

## Item Index

| Item | Type | Magical | Weight | Source |
|---|---|---|---:|---|
| [Boggart Cloak](./boggart-cloak.md) | Cloak / Armor Accessory | No | 1 stone | Rare drop from **Boggart Knifer** |

---

## Notes

- The broader item table is tracked in `umberhold/item-list.md`.
- This folder is intended for one-file-per-item authoring.

arguments.content:
# Boggart Boneboiler

## Stat Block

| Category | Details |
|---|---|
| **Name** | Boggart Boneboiler |
| **Description** | A bloated, greasy humanoid with slick skin and fat folds glistening under firelight. It reeks of burnt fat, spoiled meat, and sour sweat. Clad in a stained leather apron stretched tight over its bulk, it lumbers about with wet, slapping steps. Its small, beady eyes gleam with cruel delight as it tends to bubbling pots filled with bones and scraps, occasionally dragging victims closer to “the boil.” |
| **Level** | 2 |
| **Size** | Small (1 square on combat grid) |
| **Type** | Humanoid |
| **Alignment** | Evil |
| **AC** | 12 (leather apron) |
| **Hit Points** | 2d6 + 2 |
| **Strength** | 12 (+1) |
| **Constitution** | 12 (+1) |
| **Agility** | 10 (+0) |
| **Intellect** | 8 (-1) |
| **Piety** | 6 (-2) |
| **Personality** | 10 (+0) |
| **Luck** | 10 (+0) |
| **Proficiency Bonus** | +2 |
| **Saving Throw Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Skill Proficiencies** | None listed |
| **Damage Vulnerabilities** | None |
| **Damage Resistances** | None |
| **Damage Immunities** | None |
| **Condition Immunities** | None |
| **Attacks** | **Meat Hook:** +3 to hit, range 1, 1d6 piercing; target suffers **Bleeding** (1d4 damage at the start of its next turn). Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler lunges at (insert target) with a meat hook!* **Hurl Hot Grease:** +2 to hit, range 3, 2d4 fire damage; **recharge 6** (roll a d6 at the start of its turn and regain use on a 6); target is **Blinded** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler hurls a pot of hot grease at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Polished Finger Bone** (15%) |
| **Comments** | “Into the pot with ya!”, “Boil ’em slow, tastes better!”, “Oi, don’t waste the juices!”, “This one’ll make a fine broth!”, “Keep stirrin’, lads!”, “Boss wants this one salted!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Boneboiler has a chance to drop:
- **Polished Finger Bone** — 15%

### Polished Finger Bone Summary
A smooth, yellowed finger bone, carefully cleaned and buffed to a dull shine.
- non-magical
- worth 25 gp

### XP Note
The submitted stat block listed **25 XP**, but this entry uses **50 XP** to match the current Umberhold experience table for a **Level 2** monster.

Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler lunges at (insert target) with a meat hook!* **Hurl Hot Grease:** +2 to hit, range 3, 2d4 fire damage; **recharge 6** (roll a d6 at the start of its turn and regain use on a 6); target is **Blinded** until the end of its next turn. Combat text: *Boggart Boneboiler hurls a pot of hot grease at (insert target)!* |
| **Treasure Type** | A; **Polished Finger Bone** (15%) |
| **Comments** | “Into the pot with ya!”, “Boil ’em slow, tastes better!”, “Oi, don’t waste the juices!”, “This one’ll make a fine broth!”, “Keep stirrin’, lads!”, “Boss wants this one salted!” |
| **Experience** | 50 |

---

## Notes

### Rare Drop
In addition to its normal loot, a Boggart Boneboiler has a chance to drop:
- **Polished Finger Bone** — 15%

### Polished Finger Bone Summary
A smooth, yellowed finger bone, carefully cleaned and buffed to a dull shine.
- non-magical
- worth 25 gp

### XP Note
The submitted stat block listed **25 XP**, but this entry uses **50 XP** to match the current Umberhold experience table for a **Level 2** monster.
"}

content.text:
Coins/Currency

There are 3 coins in the game - the silver Rook (basic coin), Gold Crown and the Platinum Stag (only traded by the wealthy) 

<html>
<body>
<!--StartFragment--><html><head></head><body>
Coin Type | Value
-- | --
Silver Rook | Base coin
Gold Crown | 10 silver rooks
Platinum Stag | 25 gold crowns

</body></html><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>


::


Gemstones. These are the gems that can be found in loot tables.


<html>
<body>
<!--StartFragment--><html><head></head><body>
Value | Type | Color / Description
-- | -- | --
10 gp | Gloomshard | Dull black with faint oily sheen
  | Rotglass | Cloudy green, like spoiled fluid
  | Grave Quartz | Smoky grey with hairline fractures
50 gp | Blood Ember | Dark red, seems to glow faintly
  | Mirestone | Mud-brown with flecks of sickly yellow
  | Dusktear | Deep violet, appears wet to the touch
100 gp | Witch Amber | Golden with trapped black specks
  | Thorn Crystal | Green with jagged, shard-like growth
  | Cinder Gem | Charcoal black with glowing red veins
500 gp | Widow Opal | Pale with shifting ghostly colors
  | Night Sapphire | Deep blue, almost swallowing light
  | Paleheart Jade | Sickly green, faintly warm
1,000 gp | Kingsblood Ruby | Thick crimson, like clotted blood
  | Starvoid Pearl | Black with faint pinpricks of light
  | Dread Emerald | Dark green, unnaturally vibrant
5,000 gp | Soul Diamond | Clear but distorts shapes within
  | Abyssal Onyx | Pure black, absorbs surrounding light
  | Crownfire Gem | Burning orange with internal flame-like glow

</body></html><!--EndFragment-->
</body>
</html>

content.text:
# Umberhold Items

This folder contains individual item entries for Umberhold.

---

## Item Index

| Item | Type | Magical | Weight | Source |
|---|---|---|---:|---|
| [Boggart Cloak](./boggart-cloak.md) | Cloak / Armor Accessory | No | 1 stone | Rare drop from **Boggart Knifer** |

---

## Notes

- The broader item table is tracked in `umberhold/item-list.md`.
- This folder is intended for one-file-per-item authoring.

### Monster Open Questions
- whether monster hit points use only size dice or also add Constitution-derived bonuses by level
- whether alignment has any systemic effect beyond lore and spell interaction
- the full canonical skill list used by monsters and characters
- whether monster spellcasters follow player spell slot rules or use bespoke spellcasting packages

### Experience Open Questions
- the exact character XP thresholds required for levels 1-10
- whether benched roster members ever receive shared XP
- whether summoned allies or temporary NPC allies affect XP splitting
- whether story or quest XP exists in addition to monster XP
- whether escape or partial-victory outcomes grant reduced XP
- the exact level-up benefits by class and level

---

## 24. Source Sheets

This consolidated file is currently based on:
- `umberhold/combat-turn-canon.md`
- `umberhold/damage-canon.md`
- `umberhold/spellcasting-canon.md`
- `umberhold/spells-cantrips-1st.md`
- `umberhold/conditions-canon.md`
- `umberhold/monster-stat-block-canon.md`

These may still be kept as narrower reference sheets if useful.

# Umberhold Item List

This is a growing master item list for Umberhold.

It can include:
- mundane items
- magical items
- unique drops
- quest items
- consumables
- armor and weapons

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## Items

| Name | Type | Magical | Weight | Effect | Source / Notes |
|---|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **Boggart Cloak** | Cloak / Armor Accessory | No | 1 stone | Grants advantage on Stealth checks | Rare unique drop from **Boggart Knifer** (1 in 500). A patchwork cloak of filthy cloth, flayed skin, and scraps of wulf fur, reeking of damp rot. |
| **Bone Collar** | Collar / Trade Good | No | TBD | No confirmed gameplay effect | Rare drop from **Wulf** (1 in 25). A crude bone collar carved by Boggart masters, etched with jagged symbols. Worth 25 gp. |
| **Polished Finger Bone** | Curio / Trade Good | No | TBD | No confirmed gameplay effect | Rare drop from **Boggart Boneboiler** (15%). A smooth, yellowed finger bone, carefully cleaned and buffed to a dull shine. Worth 25 gp. |

arguments.content:
# Umberhold Currency Canon

This document captures the current approved currency system for Umberhold.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## Coin Types

There are currently **three coins** in Umberhold:

| Coin Type | Value |
|---|---|
| **Silver Rook** | Base coin |
| **Gold Crown** | 10 Silver Rooks |
| **Platinum Stag** | 25 Gold Crowns |

---

## Coin Conversion

### Base Conversion
- **1 Gold Crown** = **10 Silver Rooks**
- **1 Platinum Stag** = **25 Gold Crowns**

### Expanded Conversion
- **1 Platinum Stag** = **250 Silver Rooks**

---

## Notes

- The **Silver Rook** is the standard everyday coin.
- The **Gold Crown** is the main higher-value trade coin.
- The **Platinum Stag** is used primarily by the wealthy.

---

## Approved Rules Captured Here

This document locks in the following:
- Umberhold uses three coin types
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

---

## Still Open for Later Canonization

The following are still open and may need later clarification:
- whether prices in older notes using `gp` should now be read as **Gold Crowns**
- whether very small prices use fractional Silver Rooks or are rounded
- whether there are copper-equivalent sub-coins or not
- how shops display mixed-currency totals in the UI

---

## Implementation Note

This file is a standalone approved rules sheet.
It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

# Umberhold Gemstones

This document captures the current gemstone list for Umberhold loot tables.

These gemstones are trade valuables that can be placed into treasure tables and loot rewards.

It does **not** modify `MASTER.md`.

---

## Gemstone Table

| Value | Type | Color / Description |
|---:|---|---|
| 10 Gold Crowns | Gloomshard | Dull black with faint oily sheen |
| 10 Gold Crowns | Rotglass | Cloudy green, like spoiled fluid |
| 10 Gold Crowns | Grave Quartz | Smoky grey with hairline fractures |
| 50 Gold Crowns | Blood Ember | Dark red, seems to glow faintly |
| 50 Gold Crowns | Mirestone | Mud-brown with flecks of sickly yellow |
| 50 Gold Crowns | Dusktear | Deep violet, appears wet to the touch |
| 100 Gold Crowns | Witch Amber | Golden with trapped black specks |
| 100 Gold Crowns | Thorn Crystal | Green with jagged, shard-like growth |
| 100 Gold Crowns | Cinder Gem | Charcoal black with glowing red veins |
| 500 Gold Crowns | Widow Opal | Pale with shifting ghostly colors |
| 500 Gold Crowns | Night Sapphire | Deep blue, almost swallowing light |
| 500 Gold Crowns | Paleheart Jade | Sickly green, faintly warm |
| 1,000 Gold Crowns | Kingsblood Ruby | Thick crimson, like clotted blood |
| 1,000 Gold Crowns | Starvoid Pearl | Black with faint pinpricks of light |
| 1,000 Gold Crowns | Dread Emerald | Dark green, unnaturally vibrant |
| 5,000 Gold Crowns | Soul Diamond | Clear but distorts shapes within |
| 5,000 Gold Crowns | Abyssal Onyx | Pure black, absorbs surrounding light |
| 5,000 Gold Crowns | Crownfire Gem | Burning orange with internal flame-like glow |

---

## Value Bands

### 10 Gold Crowns
- Gloomshard
- Rotglass
- Grave Quartz

### 50 Gold Crowns
- Blood Ember
- Mirestone
- Dusktear

### 100 Gold Crowns
- Witch Amber
- Thorn Crystal
- Cinder Gem

### 500 Gold Crowns
- Widow Opal
- Night Sapphire
- Paleheart Jade

### 1,000 Gold Crowns
- Kingsblood Ruby
- Starvoid Pearl
- Dread Emerald

### 5,000 Gold Crowns
- Soul Diamond
- Abyssal Onyx
- Crownfire Gem

---

## Notes

- These are intended for loot tables and treasure rewards.
- Values are currently presented in **Gold Crowns**.
- If older notes refer to `gp`, they may need a future canon cleanup to align with named Umberhold currency.

arguments.oldText:
## 21. Experience and Leveling Access

The current approved experience rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

### Current XP Rules
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- the XP table for monster levels 1-20 is defined
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost
- full level-up rules will be defined separately

---

## 22. Approved Rules Currently Locked
arguments.newText:
## 21. Experience and Leveling Access

The current approved experience rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

### Current XP Rules
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- the XP table for monster levels 1-20 is defined
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost
- full level-up rules will be defined separately

---

## 22. Currency and Gemstones

The current approved currency rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/currency-canon.md`

The current gemstone reference is maintained in:
- `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`

### Current Currency Rules
- Umberhold uses three coin types: Silver Rook, Gold Crown, and Platinum Stag
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

### Current Gemstone Rules
- gemstones are valid loot-table valuables
- gemstone values are currently tracked in Gold Crowns

---

## 23. Approved Rules Currently Locked

","newText":"## 21. Experience and Leveling Access

The current approved experience rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/experience-canon.md`

### Current XP Rules
- monsters award XP based on monster level
- the XP table for monster levels 1-20 is defined
- XP is shared equally among surviving party members
- leveling up is performed at an inn
- leveling up has no gold cost
- full level-up rules will be defined separately

---

## 22. Currency and Gemstones

The current approved currency rules are maintained in:
- `umberhold/currency-canon.md`

The current gemstone reference is maintained in:
- `umberhold/items/gemstones.md`

### Current Currency Rules
- Umberhold uses three coin types: Silver Rook, Gold Crown, and Platinum Stag
- Silver Rook is the base coin
- Gold Crown is worth 10 Silver Rooks
- Platinum Stag is worth 25 Gold Crowns

### Current Gemstone Rules
- gemstones are valid loot-table valuables
- gemstone values are currently tracked in Gold Crowns

---

arguments.oldText:
### Experience Open Questions
- the exact character XP thresholds required for levels 1-10
- whether benched roster members ever receive shared XP
- whether summoned allies or temporary NPC allies affect XP splitting
- whether story or quest XP exists in addition to monster XP
- whether escape or partial-victory outcomes grant reduced XP
- the exact level-up benefits by class and level

---

## 24. Source Sheets
arguments.newText:
### Experience Open Questions
- the exact character XP thresholds required for levels 1-10
- whether benched roster members ever receive shared XP
- whether summoned allies or temporary NPC allies affect XP splitting
- whether story or quest XP exists in addition to monster XP
- whether escape or partial-victory outcomes grant reduced XP
- the exact level-up benefits by class and level

### Currency and Gemstone Open Questions
- whether older `gp` prices in existing notes should be universally converted to Gold Crowns
- whether shops ever display prices directly in mixed denominations
- whether gemstones can have special uses beyond sale value, such as quest, ritual, or crafting functions
- whether gemstones stack in inventory or remain individual items under no-stacking rules

---
